scope: Google Search Console only (bariatriccounselingcenter.com), derived from 41 GSC snapshots 2026-05-07 -> 2026-06-29 plus the 75-keyword master list. Competitor #1-blocker and AI-visibility sections are intentionally NOT auto-generated and remain manual.
wins: Both core San Antonio commercial terms hold the #2-3 tier: 'bariatric counseling san antonio' at pos 2.0 and 'weight loss counseling san antonio' at pos 2.0 (local-primary).
'san antonio bariatric center' posted the strongest positive move, delta +17.6 to pos 13.5 (4 impressions); 'bmi center san antonio' improved +6.8 to pos 11.2 (5 impressions).
'bariatric counseling' (national-primary) is the most-impressed tracked term holding page 1, pos 11.9 on 32 impressions (delta +0.9 - stable).
'bariatric therapist' improved +3.7 to pos 4.0 and 'bariatric counselor' +2.8 to pos 7.0, keeping the bariatric-specific cluster in the #4-10 tier.

Blog content drives the impression base at near-page-1 ranks: 'better-sleep-for-weight-loss' 1,359 impressions @ pos 14.8, 'how-to-improve-self-esteem-while-losing-weight' 763 @ pos 8.7, and the weight-loss-medications (Wegovy/Ozempic/Zepbound) page 831 @ pos 11.1.
needs_attention: Keyword coverage (a KEYWORD count, not a page count): 24 of 75 tracked keywords show GSC impressions; 51 show none. The 51 are mostly low-priority/long-tail terms not yet earning impressions, NOT unpublished pages. Only 2 of 48 distinct tracker target pages still point at staging, and per the 2026-06-03 sitemap check most staging-targeted tracker URLs are stale pointers to pages already live on www - a tracker-hygiene item, not lost traffic.

Visibility drift across the timeline: daily impressions fell from 2,447 (2026-05-07) to 1,447 (2026-06-29) and impression-weighted avg position moved from 14.87 to 31.49. CAVEAT: single-impression keyword positions swing heavily day to day (e.g. the prior 06-25 snapshot showed avg pos 21.38), so this is directional only, not a confirmed ranking loss.
Near-zero CTR: only 3 clicks against 1,447 impressions on the latest snapshot (~0.2%). High-impression blog posts are ranking but not converting impressions to visits.

Commercial terms with real impressions that slipped: 'weight loss counseling or therapy' delta -11.0 to pos 15.7 (7 impressions), 'eating disorder counseling san antonio' -4.3 to pos 19.2 (14 impressions), and 'eating disorder therapist san antonio' -3.6 to pos 25.4 (11 impressions).
Position distribution: 0 terms at #1, 2 at #2-3, 5 at #4-10, 8 at #11-20, 9 at 21+, 51 not ranking - the bulk of tracked terms still sit on page 2 or beyond.
priority_actions: Tracker hygiene: repoint the 2 remaining staging targetUrls in keywords-master.json ('what-is-a-bariatric-counselor' and 'what-is-a-weight-loss-counselor' on staging12) to their correct live www URLs so GSC can match them.

Fix the ~0.2% CTR by adding clear CTAs and internal links from the top-impression blog posts (better-sleep 1,359 imp; self-esteem 763 imp; weight-loss-medications 831 imp) into the matching San Antonio service pages.
On-page refresh + internal links for the slipped commercial terms, prioritizing 'eating disorder counseling san antonio' (14 imp) and 'weight loss counseling or therapy' (-11.0).
Defend the two #2-3 San Antonio local terms ('bariatric counseling san antonio', 'weight loss counseling san antonio') - they are the core money terms and currently the only top-3 holdings.
Add/verify FAQ schema on the high-impression service and blog pages for rich-result and AI-overview eligibility.
If a staging->prod publish backlog is suspected, confirm the real push list via a staging-vs-prod sitemap diff or Daniel's own notes rather than inferring from keyword/GSC data.
summary: Core San Antonio terms hold #2-3 and the bariatric cluster sits #4-10, but daily impressions and avg position drifted down (directional, single-impression swing) and CTR is ~0.2% - the top priority is converting high-impression blog traffic into service-page visits via CTAs/internal links while defending the two top-3 local terms. Scope is GSC-only; competitor and AI-visibility analysis remain manual.
